description_long: This object is a cylindrical medicine container, most likely a prescription or over-the-counter pill bottle, commonly found in pharmacies and healthcare settings. The body of the container is matte black, with a slightly wider, flat, circular lid and a matching base, both in a copper or bronze color. The lid appears to be a snap-on or screw-on type, providing secure closure to protect the contents inside. The container is opaque, ensuring the medicine inside is shielded from light exposure. Its size and shape suggest it is designed for portability and easy handling, suitable for carrying pills or capsules. There are no visible labels, text, or branding on the exterior, giving it a generic appearance.
description: A matte black cylindrical medicine bottle with a copper-colored lid and base, designed for secure storage of pills or capsules. The container is opaque and unbranded.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full circular lid and the cylindrical body, with the copper-colored rim and base clearly visible.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the smooth, uninterrupted black surface of the body and the copper accents at the top and bottom.
description_view_2: Another side view, nearly identical to view 1, emphasizing the uniformity of the container's cylindrical shape.
description_view_3: The rear view mirrors the front and side views, with the same copper and black color scheme and no visible markings.
